title: moraea viscaria
intro: Moraea viscaria is a type of plant species in the Iridaceae family, commonly known as a sticky or hairy iris, due to its sticky sap and hairy leaves. Traditionally, Moraea viscaria has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its saponins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Moraea viscaria include its potential to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues, fever, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. Moraea viscaria can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal agent.
